evidence_items.snippet: Membrane type: human skin.
Receptor medium pH 7.4 with surfactant.
Dose type: finite; Dose amount: unknown.
Foam vehicle.

Device: foam dressing.

provenance.route_notes: Evidence extraction from various analyses comparing ibuprofen foam dressings and their effectiveness.

route_anchor_evidence.snippet: the pain measures and time frames reported were different. One trial indicated that pain relief achieved over 5 days with ibuprofen dressings could represent a clinically relevant reduction in pain.

evidence_items.snippet: SilatosTM silicone membrane of thickness 0.13 mm was purchased from Atos Medical, Sweden.
Phosphate buffer (pH 7.2) was used as the release media in the receptor compartment.
approximately 1 mL of the IBU gel administered by filling the quartz tube.
Ibuprofen was purchased from TCI Chemicals Ltd, Oxford, UK.
A 5 % w/w ibuprofen gel formulation was used as the model topical formulation.
Average IBU amount in µg/mL permeated vs time.
The experiment was conducted for 12 h in triplicate at 37 °C.
providing a diffusion area of 3.14 cm2.
The 3D printed diffusion cell proved useful in allowing the visualisation of the permeation process.

provenance.route_notes: Study showcases a 3D printed Franz cell with UV imaging to characterise ibuprofen permeation through a silicone membrane.

route_anchor_evidence.locator: Page 11, section 2.2.6 (UV imaging of the permeation process)
route_anchor_evidence.snippet: The absorbance readings at 30 s intervals were extracted from the software... to provide a quantitative assessment of IBU permeated through the membrane.
A 5 % w/w ibuprofen gel formulation was used as the model topical formulation.

evidence_items.snippet: EBOV GP, and high-resolution complexes of GP with the anticancer drug toremifene and the painkiller ibuprofen.
endosomal entry and membrane fusion.
marginal effect.
both toremifene and ibuprofen.
less potent inhibitor.
Thermal shift assays show up to a 14 °C decrease in the protein melting temperature after toremifene binding.
premature release of GP2.
cavity between the attachment (GP1) and fusion (GP2) subunits.
links with equivalent tunnels from the other monomers.

route_anchor_evidence.snippet: Thermal shift assays show up to a 14 °C decrease in the protein melting temperature after toremifene binding, while ibuprofen has only a marginal effect and is a less potent inhibitor.

route_raw_labels.endpoint_carrier_snippet: Thermal shift assays show up to a 14 °C decrease in the protein melting temperature after toremifene binding, while ibuprofen has only a marginal effect and is a less potent inhibitor.

source_notes: The role of ibuprofen is described as a marginally effective inhibitor in the context of GP destabilization.

evidence_items.snippet: Membrane-type was human skin.
Receptor medium was physiological saline with pH 7.4.
400 mg ibuprofen and 1000 mg acetaminophen.
Composed of water for the administration.
400 mg ibuprofen administered.
Significantly lower peak TC in IBU/APAP compared to CTRL.
Measurements recorded every 20 seconds.
Diffusion area specified as human skin.
Utilized telemetric temperature pill.

provenance.route_notes: The study effectively demonstrates the impact of ibuprofen on core body temperature during exercise, but no diffusion cell methodology is used.

route_anchor_evidence.snippet: Table 1. Comparison of exercise test results across conditions showing lower maximum TC in IBU/APAP vs. CTRL.
Subjects received 400mg ibuprofen (IBU) with acetaminophen (APAP) during the sub-maximal tests.
